61.reason: [The condition has incorrect operator precedence; it should be (cur == '&' && next != '&') || (cur == '|' && next != '|'). Also, after detecting an error, it incorrectly creates a token for "&&" regardless of whether the current character is '&' or '|', and only advances by one character instead of handling both cases properly.]

64.reason: [This always uses "&" even when the error is caused by a single '|', which is incorrect.]

65.reason: [This tries to create a token for "&&" even when the input is just "&" or "|", which is wrong behavior for an erroneous input. According to the problem, erroneous programs should only output error information, not tokens.]
